text:
REGIME — Broad-based grind higher, defensives led, metals lagged, breadth modest.

INDICES — VIX 24.2, falling. QQQ +0.60%, IJR +0.54%, SPY +0.47%, IWM +0.43%, DIA +0.37% best to worst. Small caps outperformed large caps on the day. VIXY dropped -2.45%, the sharpest cross-asset move, confirming fear unwinding.

SECTORS — XLP +0.94%, XLY +0.82%, XLE +0.73%, XLF +0.71%, XLK +0.58%, XLI +0.51%, XLRE +0.36%, XLC +0.05%, XLV -0.36%, XLU -0.37%, XLB -0.38%. Top industries: MSOS +6.23%, IBIT +4.08%. Bottom industries: TAN -1.26%, LIT -1.42%.

THE 7s — Defense +1.25%, Fab +1.22%, Housing +1.08%, Toll Road +1.00%, Semi +0.84%, Bank +0.73%, Industrial +0.66%, Energy +0.57%, New Staples +0.51%, Old Staples +0.45%, Insurance +0.44%, Freight +0.35%, Cloud +0.19%, Old Metals +0.05%, Medtech -0.16%, Power -0.27%, Health -0.39%, Buildout -0.40%, Software -0.63%, New Metals -0.85%. Strongest ticker in Defense 7 not specified; weakest in New Metals 7 not specified from data.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.4, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +6.23% (Industries), IBIT +4.08% (Industries), EATZ +2.02% (Industries, 10.4x vol). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +6.19%, worst XLC -4.55%; best basket Energy +8.57%, worst Software -10.72%. Yield decline on growth fears from Brent at $121.88 drove the tech rebound, with QQQ leading indices.

WATCH — EATZ: +2.02%, 10.4x average volume warrants follow-through check. IJK: +0.28%, 8.0x vol spike with minimal price move signals positioning without confirmation. MSOS: +6.23%, momentum break after persistent underperformance deserves catalyst verification.
